qtype: money calculation, working with rates, ratio in lowest terms
tex:
These are the rates charged by a painter, a plumber and an electrician who do some work for Mr Sharma.
\newline

(a) The painter works for 7 hours.
\newline

Calculate the amount Mr Sharma pays the painter.
\newline

\$ 

(b) Mr Sharma pays the plumber \$252.
\newline

Calculate how many hours the plumber works.
\newline

 hours

(c) Mr Sharma pays the electrician \$224.
\newline

Calculate how many hours the electrician works.
\newline

 hours

(d) Write down the ratio of the amount Mr Sharma pays to the painter, the plumber and the electrician.
\newline
Give your answer in its lowest terms.
\newline

tex:
(a) A shop gives each of 1000 people a voucher.
\newline
28 people use their voucher.
\newline
The shop now gives each of $16\,500$ people a voucher.
\newline

Calculate how many of these $16\,500$ people are expected to use their voucher.
\newline

(b) In a class activity, all the 15 students wear hats.
\newline
7 students wear red hats, 6 students wear green hats and 2 students wear white hats.
\newline

(i) (c) One of the students is picked at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this student wears a red hat.
\newline

(d) Two of the 15 students are picked at random.
\newline

Show that the probability that these two students wear hats of the same colour is
$\frac{37}{105}$.
\newline

(e) Three of the 15 students are picked at random.
\newline

Find the probability that at least two of these three students wear red hats.
\newline

content.content:
A shop gives each of 1000 people a voucher.
\newline
28 people use their voucher.
\newline
The shop now gives each of $16\,500$ people a voucher.
\newline

Calculate how many of these $16\,500$ people are expected to use their voucher.
\newline

In a class activity, all the 15 students wear hats.
\newline
7 students wear red hats, 6 students wear green hats and 2 students wear white hats.
\newline

One of the students is picked at random.
\newline

Find the probability that this student wears a red hat.
\newline

Two of the 15 students are picked at random.
\newline

Show that the probability that these two students wear hats of the same colour is
$\frac{37}{105}$.
\newline

Three of the 15 students are picked at random.
\newline

Find the probability that at least two of these three students wear red hats.
\newline

qtype: density calculation, percentage usage, reverse percentage, currency conversion
tex:
(a) A box is a cuboid with length 45 cm, width 30 cm and height 42 cm.
\newline
The box is completely filled with 90.72 kg of sand.
\newline

Calculate the density of this sand in kg/m$^3$.
\newline

[Density = mass / volume]

 kg/m$^3$

(b) A bag contains $15\,000$ cm$^3$ of sand.
\newline
Some of this sand is used to completely fill a hole in the shape of a cylinder.
\newline
The hole is 30 cm deep and has radius 10 cm.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of the sand from the bag that is used.
\newline

\%

(c) Sand costs \$98.90 per tonne.
\newline
This cost includes a tax of 15\%.
\newline

Calculate the amount of tax paid per tonne of sand.
\newline

\$ 

(d) Raj buys some sand for 3540 rupees.
\newline

Calculate the cost in dollars when the exchange rate is \$1 = 70.8 rupees.
\newline

\$

content.content:
A box is a cuboid with length 45 cm, width 30 cm and height 42 cm.
\newline
The box is completely filled with 90.72 kg of sand.
\newline

Calculate the density of this sand in kg/m$^3$.
\newline

[Density = mass / volume]

A bag contains $15\,000$ cm$^3$ of sand.
\newline
Some of this sand is used to completely fill a hole in the shape of a cylinder.
\newline
The hole is 30 cm deep and has radius 10 cm.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[]

\$ \AnswerLine[]

_rawBody:
\begin{parts}
\item A box is a cuboid with length 45 cm, width 30 cm and height 42 cm.
The box is completely filled with 90.72 kg of sand.

Calculate the density of this sand in kg/m$^3$.

[Density = mass / volume]

\AnswerLine[] kg/m$^3$
\Marks{3}

\item A bag contains 15\,000 cm$^3$ of sand.
Some of this sand is used to completely fill a hole in the shape of a cylinder.
The hole is 30 cm deep and has radius 10 cm.

Calculate the percentage of the sand from the bag that is used.

\AnswerLine[]\%
\Marks{3}

\item Sand costs \$98.90 per tonne.
This cost includes a tax of 15\%.

Calculate the amount of tax paid per tonne of sand.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}

\item Raj buys some sand for 3540 rupees.

Calculate the cost in dollars when the exchange rate is \$1 = 70.8 rupees.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}
\end{parts}
